methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: DEEPX
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series C), post-money $529M

data.narrative:
Nearfield Instruments is a Series C-stage semiconductor company that has raised €135M+ to date. Valuation analysis draws from a universe of 4 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private peers (SambaNova Systems at $5.0B in Series D, Tenstorrent at $2.7B in Series D, Positron AI at $1.0B in Series B, and DEEPX at $529M in Series C), alongside 3 public semiconductor comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) trading at a median P/S multiple of 24.4x.

The peer-median method is the sole applicable triangulation approach here. The 4 private comparables yield a median valuation of $1.9B, with a range spanning $529M to $5.0B. This method directly reflects market pricing for similarly staged semiconductor ventures. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied because Nearfield's revenue is not disclosed, which is typical for private-company filings.

Analysis supports a valuation range of $1.6B to $2.1B, centered on the peer median of $1.9B. The spread reflects the wide dispersion in comparable valuations—particularly the gap between early-stage Series B ($1.0B) and later-stage Series D ($2.7B–$5.0B) peers. Key uncertainties include Nearfield's revenue trajectory, product differentiation relative to the comparables set, and capital efficiency relative to peers at different funding stages. The single-method triangulation and limited private-comp sample size warrant cautious application of this range.
